regional.title: Traffic Analysis Districts
regional.copy: The regional report groups traffic counts into Traffic Analysis Districts, small areas bounded by major roads. This makes it possible to compare change across urban, suburban, and exurban parts of the metro.

regional.title: Vehicle miles traveled
regional.copy: VMT combines road length and traffic volume. The report estimates regional VMT grew 13.4% from 2020 to 2022 after an estimated 8% decrease from 2018 to 2020.

regional.title: Post-2020 recovery
regional.copy: Growth was strongest in suburban and exurban areas. The Omaha central business district was an exception, with a reported 2% decrease from 2020 to 2022.

regional.title: Continuous counters
regional.copy: The MAPA region has 15 active continuous traffic counters, including 10 in Nebraska and 5 in Iowa. Ten have been active since 1994 and help show long-term travel patterns.

intersections.title: Busiest location
intersections.copy: 90th Street & West Dodge Road remains the busiest reported intersection, with 91,000 annual average daily vehicles.

intersections.title: Omaha concentration
intersections.copy: The report notes that nearly all of the top 50 regional intersections are within or shared by the City of Omaha boundary.

intersections.title: County highlights
intersections.copy: 84th Street & Harrison Street is the highest-ranking Sarpy County intersection. South Expressway & I-80/I-29 westbound ramps leads Pottawattamie County.

interchanges.title: Interstate loop activity
interchanges.copy: Interchange volumes are highest along the I-80/I-680 loop in Omaha. I-680 & I-80 is the top-ranked interchange with 231,000 AADT.

interchanges.title: County highlights
interchanges.copy: The report identifies 126th-Harrison Street & I-80 as the highest-volume Sarpy County interchange and I-29 West & I-80 as the highest-volume Pottawattamie County interchange.

freight.title: Freight Analysis Framework
freight.copy: The report uses FAF5 to understand freight movement by mode and truck-flow estimates across the National Highway System and National Highway Freight Network.

freight.title: Pass-through freight
freight.copy: The 2017 model shows 3,928,463 thousand tons of freight crossing FAF Region 311 network roads, while 87,046 thousand tons, or 2.2%, originated from, ended in, or stayed within the region.

freight.title: 2050 truck growth
freight.copy: Daily truck volumes are projected to grow by 76% within the FAF 311 region by 2050.

multimodal.title: Bike and pedestrian counts
multimodal.copy: MAPA and the City of Omaha use six permanent counters to monitor bicycle and pedestrian activity, with comparable coverage beginning in 2019.

multimodal.title: Strava activity
multimodal.copy: Strava Metroview provides a large but sampled view of public activity. In 2022, about 8% of regional Strava activities were flagged as commutes.

multimodal.title: Transit and ORBT
multimodal.copy: Metro automated passenger count data is summarized by Traffic Analysis District. ORBT serves as a spine of the transit system and accounts for about one-fifth of Metro ridership.

multimodal.title: Other modes
multimodal.copy: Heartland Bike Share operated 91 stations in 2022, and 141,673 e-scooter trips were recorded, primarily around Downtown, Midtown, Aksarben, and Benson.
